item.title: A conversation with a prediction-market prophet
item.description: Tarek Mansour, the CEO of Kalshi, thinks his company can fundamentally change how you receive news and information.

item.title: Government overreach won’t fix youth gender medicine
item.description: The real cure for bad science is better clinical trials, not lawsuits.

item.title: What Bucha taught me about Ukraine’s will to fight
item.description: Two cities — one a massacre site, one under fire — reveal why Ukrainians won't trade land for peace.

item.title: The federal government's big fat real estate problem
item.description: Empty office buildings in prime locations stifle local economies.

item.title: What Venezuela has hidden for 27 years — and counting
item.description: Twin earthquakes expose the deadly cost of a system built on lies.

item.title: How Poland’s fate drove the Declaration
item.description: Poland was carved up in 1772. Four years later, Americans took note.

item.title: To beat socialists and populists, liberalism must get radical
item.description: It became the ideology of the establishment it once existed to attack. That’s why it’s losing.
